Developing Streamlit App Functions

Developing Streamlit App Functions#

import seaborn as sns

import pandas as pd

pio.templates.default='ggplot2'

def plot_stocks_df(df_,x="Date", stocks=None):
    """Plots the stock columns in the dataframe."""
    df = df_.copy()
    if df.index.name==x:
        df.reset_index(inplace=True)
    if stocks is None:
        stocks = list(df.drop(columns=x).columns)


    fig =  px.line(df, x='Date', y=stocks)
    fig.update_layout(
    xaxis=go.layout.XAxis(

        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
    #                     dict(count=1,
    #                         label="1m",
    #                         step="month",
    #                         stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6,
                    label="6m",
                    step="month",
                    stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1,
                    label="YTD",
                    step="year",
                    stepmode="todate"),
                dict(count=1,
                    label="1y",
                    step="year",
                    stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all")
            ])
        ),
        rangeslider=dict(
            visible=True
        ),
        type="date"
    ),

    yaxis = go.layout.YAxis(
                autorange=True,
                title=go.layout.yaxis.Title(
                    text = 'Price',
                    font=dict(
                        # family="Courier New, monospace",
                        size=18,
                        color="#7f7f7f")
                )
        )
    )
    return fig

plot_stocks_df(df)

def get_technical_indicators(dataset_,make_price_from='Adj Close',days=1):
    
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
#     dataset =  dataset_.copy()
    dataset= pd.DataFrame(index=dataset_.index)
    dataset['price'] = dataset_[make_price_from].copy()
        
    # Create 7 and 21 days Moving Average
    dataset['ma7'] = dataset['price'].rolling(window=7*days).mean()
    dataset['ma21'] = dataset['price'].rolling(window=21*days).mean()
    
    # Create MACD
    dataset['26ema'] = dataset['price'].ewm(span=26*days).mean()
#     dataset['12ema'] = pd.ewma(dataset['price'], span=12)
    dataset['12ema'] = dataset['price'].ewm(span=12*days).mean()

    dataset['MACD'] = (dataset['12ema']-dataset['26ema'])

    # Create Bollinger Bands
#     dataset['20sd'] = pd.stats.moments.rolling_std(dataset['price'],20)
    dataset['20sd'] = dataset['price'].rolling(20*days).std()
    dataset['upper_band'] = dataset['ma21'] + (dataset['20sd']*2)
    dataset['lower_band'] = dataset['ma21'] - (dataset['20sd']*2)
    
    # Create Exponential moving average
    dataset['ema'] = dataset['price'].ewm(com=0.5).mean()
    
    # Create Momentum
    dataset['momentum'] = dataset['price']-days*1
    
    return dataset#.drop(columns=)

fig = af.plot_stocks_df(stock_df)
fig

def update_layout_daterange_slider(fig):
    # Add range slider
    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis=go.layout.XAxis(

            rangeselector=dict(
                buttons=list([
#                     dict(count=1,
#                         label="1m",
#                         step="month",
#                         stepmode="backward"),
                    dict(count=6,
                        label="6m",
                        step="month",
                        stepmode="backward"),
                    dict(count=1,
                        label="YTD",
                        step="year",
                        stepmode="todate"),
                    dict(count=1,
                        label="1y",
                        step="year",
                        stepmode="backward"),
                    dict(step="all")
                ])
            ),
            rangeslider=dict(
                visible=True
            ),
            type="date"
        ),

        yaxis = go.layout.YAxis(
                    autorange=True,
                    title=go.layout.yaxis.Title(
                        text = 'Price',
                        font=dict(
                            # family="Courier New, monospace",
                            size=18,
                            color="#7f7f7f")
                    )
            )
        )
    return fig

update_layout_daterange_slider(fig)

def plot_time_series(stock_df,x='Date', plot_cols=['price', 'ma7', 'ma21', 
                                                     '26ema', '12ema', 'MACD', 
                                                     '20sd', 'upper_band',
                                                     'lower_band', 'ema',
                                                     'momentum'],
                      title='Stock Price + Technical Indicators'):
    fig = px.line(stock_df,x=x,y=plot_cols,title=title)
    ## Add slider and buttons
    fig = update_layout_daterange_slider(fig)
    return fig
    
fig = plot_time_series(stock_df)
fig

fig = plot_time_series(stock_df)
fig

print(fig)

fig['data']

# FIND THE PRICE TRACE AND CHANGE ITS PROPERTIES, PUT IT ON TOP
temp_data = list(fig['data'])

## search traces for correct name
for i,trace in enumerate(temp_data):#fig['data']):

    if 'price' in trace['name']:

        temp_price = temp_data.pop(i)
        temp_price['line']['width'] = 3
        temp_price['line']['color'] = 'orange'
        # temp_price['line']['dash'] = 'dot'

        temp_data.append(temp_price)
        break

fig['data'] = tuple(temp_data)
# Reverse legend so last entry (price) is on top
fig['layout']['legend']['traceorder'] = 'reversed'
fig

def plot_time_series(stock_df,x='Date', plot_cols=['price', 'ma7', 'ma21', 
                                                     '26ema', '12ema', 'MACD', 
                                                     '20sd', 'upper_band',
                                                     'lower_band', 'ema',
                                                     'momentum'],
                      title='Stock Price + Technical Indicators'):
    fig = px.line(stock_df,x=x,y=plot_cols,title=title)
    ## Add slider and buttons
    fig = update_layout_daterange_slider(fig)
    


    # FIND THE PRICE TRACE AND CHANGE ITS PROPERTIES, PUT IT ON TOP
    temp_data = list(fig['data'])

    ## search traces for correct name
    for i,trace in enumerate(temp_data):#fig['data']):

        if 'price' in trace['name']:

            temp_price = temp_data.pop(i)
            temp_price['line']['width'] = 3
            temp_price['line']['color'] = 'orange'
            # temp_price['line']['dash'] = 'dot'

            temp_data.append(temp_price)
            break

    fig['data'] = tuple(temp_data)
    # Reverse legend so last entry (price) is on top
    fig['layout']['legend']['traceorder'] = 'reversed'
    return fig


fig = plot_time_series(stock_df)
fig
